name: mpmath (Arbitrary-Precision Floating-Point Arithmetic)

performance.notes: Speed proportional to precision; 10-1000x slower than float64 at high precision

agent_readiness.notes: Arbitrary-precision floating-point arithmetic for Python. from mpmath import mp, mpf, pi, sqrt, nsum, inf. mp.dps = 50 (decimal places). mp.prec = 200 (binary bits). x = mpf('1.5'). pi_100 = mp.pi. sin_val = mp.sin(mpf('1.5')). High-precision: mp.nstr(pi, 50). Integration: mp.quad(f, [0, 1]). ODE: mp.odefun. Infinite series: mp.nsum. Used internally by SymPy for numerical evaluation. Excellent numerical special functions (Bessel, hypergeometric, etc.).

gotchas: Much slower than float64 — only use when precision is required
mp.dps is global state — not thread-safe without context management
Binary precision (mp.prec) vs decimal places (mp.dps) distinction causes confusion
best_when: Verifying floating-point algorithms with ground-truth precision
Special function evaluation at arbitrary precision
Numerical integration and ODE solving at high precision
avoid_when: Standard scientific computing (numpy float64 is sufficient)
Array operations (mpmath is scalar; no array type)
summary: mpmath provides arbitrary-precision floating-point arithmetic. mp.dps = 50 sets 50 decimal places. mpf('1.5'), mp.pi, mp.sin(x). Quadrature: mp.quad(f, [a, b]). Infinite series: mp.nsum(f, [0, inf]). Used by SymPy for numerical evaluation. Excellent special functions. BSD-3. Slower than float64 by 10-1000x. mp.dps is global state. Best for high-precision verification and numerical methods where float64 is insufficient.
